title: TheRealReal Q1 2026 slides: AI drives margin gains, outlook raised
text: TheRealReal exceeded expectations in Q1 2026, reporting revenue of $190 million (vs. $188M consensus) and an EPS loss of just $0.01 (better than $0.05 forecast). Gross merchandise volume reached $606 million, representing 24% year-over-year growth. The company achieved significant operational leverage through artificial intelligence initiatives. Operating expenses improved 730 basis points as a percentage of revenue, with adjusted EBITDA nearly tripling to $13.1 million (6.9% of revenue) from $4.1 million in Q1 2025. Key efficiency gains included: marketing expenses held flat at 9.8% of revenue, operations and technology costs declined from 41.9% to 38.3%, SG&A expenses improved from 31.2% to 27.6%. Management attributed improvements to AI and automation initiatives, enhanced sales and retail team productivity, and fixed cost leverage from revenue growth. The company leverages 15 years of accumulated data on 50+ million items to power sophisticated pricing algorithms. Management highlighted the Athena AI system for intake processes designed to reduce consignment friction. For full-year 2026, TheRealReal projects GMV of $2.42-$2.47 billion, revenue of $770-$784 million, and adjusted EBITDA of $59-$67 million (approximately 8% margin at midpoint). Average order value increased 15% to $646. Trailing twelve-month active buyer count reached 1.08 million, up 10% year-over-year. 43% of new sellers originated from existing buyer base.
